items.q: In basic kitchen terms, what is 'tool used to check doneness temperature'?
items.explanation: tool used to check doneness temperature corresponds to probe thermometer.

items.q: In kitchen basics, what is 'base mixture of chopped onion, carrot, and celery'?
In basic kitchen terms, what is '1 tablespoon in metric volume'?
items.explanation: 1 tablespoon in metric volume corresponds to 15 mL.

items.q: In basic kitchen terms, what is 'to cook in liquid just below boiling'? (set 2)
items.explanation: to cook in liquid just below boiling corresponds to poach.

items.q: A probe thermometer helps verify doneness.
In basic kitchen terms, what is 'dry heat cooking in an oven'?
items.explanation: dry heat cooking in an oven corresponds to baking or roasting.

items.q: In basic kitchen terms, what is 'safe internal temperature for chicken'?
items.explanation: safe internal temperature for chicken corresponds to 75C.

items.q: In basic kitchen terms, what is 'rapid cooling of hot food prevents'?
items.explanation: rapid cooling of hot food prevents corresponds to bacterial growth.

items.q: In basic kitchen terms, what is 'emulsion of oil and vinegar'?
items.explanation: emulsion of oil and vinegar corresponds to vinaigrette.

items.q: Sauteing uses low heat and lots of liquid.
